name: Trade routes, roads, and campaign corridors

properties.period: Iron Age / Saul-David era with all-period physical corridors
source_notes: Routes are schematic campaign corridors, not surveyed road alignments.
Linework follows durable physical corridors: coast road, ridge road, Jordan valley, Jezreel, Shephelah valleys, wilderness tracks, and Transjordan plateau routes.

properties.name: Coastal trunk road / Via Maris corridor
properties.notes: Fast north-south coastal route linking Egypt, Philistia, Sharon, Carmel, and Phoenicia.

properties.name: Philistia to Judah valley roads
properties.notes: Shephelah east-west valleys toward Judah: Sorek, Elah, Lachish, and Guvrin style corridors.

properties.name: Jordan Valley road
properties.notes: North-south rift movement from Kinneret/Beth-shean through Jericho to the Dead Sea basin.

properties.name: Jericho to Benjamin/Jebus ascent
properties.notes: Steep ascent from the Rift to the Benjamin/Jerusalem ridge.

properties.name: Transjordan plateau road / King's Highway corridor
properties.notes: Schematic plateau route through Ammon, Moab, and Edom edge.

properties.name: Negev wilderness track
properties.notes: Sparse-water southern track linking Hebron, Beersheba, Ziklag, Kadesh-barnea, and Sinai approaches.

properties.name: Jezreel crossroads
properties.notes: Carmel/Megiddo/Beth-shean corridor connecting coast, Galilee, and Jordan.
